label: Very long email body (>3000 chars) - should truncate gracefully

data.subject: Q2 Planning Document Review Needed
data.textBody:
Hi team,

Please review the Q2 planning document by April 15th. Here are the details:

Section 1: Revenue Targets
We are projecting a 15% increase in revenue for Q2 based on the following assumptions. First, our enterprise pipeline has grown by 23% compared to Q1. Second, the new pricing tier launched in March is showing strong adoption with 47 customers upgrading in the first two weeks. Third, churn rate has decreased from 4.2% to 3.1% month-over-month.

The breakdown by product line is as follows:
- Core Platform: $2.4M (up from $2.1M)
- Analytics Add-on: $890K (up from $720K)
- Enterprise Suite: $1.6M (up from $1.2M)
- Professional Services: $450K (stable)

Section 2: Engineering Roadmap
The engineering team has committed to the following deliverables for Q2:

Sprint 1 (April 1-14): Complete the migration to the new authentication provider. This includes updating all OAuth flows, migrating existing user sessions, and implementing the new MFA system. Dependencies include the security audit completion and the updated compliance documentation.

Sprint 2 (April 15-28): Launch the real-time collaboration feature. This has been in beta since January and feedback from the 50 beta testers has been overwhelmingly positive. Key metrics: 92% satisfaction rate, average session duration increased by 34%, and zero critical bugs reported in the last two weeks.

Sprint 3 (April 29 - May 12): Begin the API v3 development. This is a major undertaking that will span multiple quarters. For Q2, the goal is to complete the design specification, set up the new endpoint infrastructure, and implement the first batch of endpoints covering user management and project CRUD operations.

Sprint 4 (May 13-26): Focus on performance optimization. Our P95 latency has crept up to 450ms and we need to get it back under 200ms. The main bottlenecks identified are: database query optimization (particularly the dashboard aggregation queries), caching layer improvements, and CDN configuration for static assets.

Sprint 5 (May 27 - June 9): Quality and stability sprint. This includes addressing the top 20 customer-reported bugs, improving test coverage from 72% to 85%, and conducting a full security audit of the new features.

Sprint 6 (June 10-23): Final polish and Q2 wrap-up. Feature freeze on June 15th, followed by a week of regression testing and documentation updates.

Section 3: Hiring Plan
We have approval to hire 8 new positions in Q2:
- 3 Senior Backend Engineers
- 2 Frontend Engineers
- 1 DevOps Engineer
- 1 Product Designer
- 1 Technical Writer

Current pipeline status: 45 active candidates, 12 in technical interview stage, 3 offers extended (2 accepted, 1 pending).

Section 4: Budget
Total Q2 budget: $4.2M
- Engineering: $2.8M (salaries, tools, infrastructure)
- Marketing: $650K (events, content, ads)
- Sales: $500K (travel, commissions, tools)
- Operations: $250K (office, admin, legal)

Section 5: Key Risks
1. The authentication migration has a tight timeline and any delays could impact Sprint 2.
2. Two key engineers are on parental leave during May, which reduces capacity by approximately 15%.
3. The API v3 design may require breaking changes that affect existing integrations.
4. AWS costs have been trending 12% above forecast due to increased usage.

Please review and provide feedback by April 15th so we can finalize at the leadership offsite.

Best regards,
Diana
